description: Temporary price movement beyond a key swing high or low designed to trigger stop-loss orders and gather liquidity before reversing.
A deliberate market movement to trigger stop-loss orders clustered around obvious support or resistance levels.
A false upside breakout that induces retail traders to buy, only to reverse sharply downward and trap them.
A false downside breakout that induces retail traders to short, only to reverse sharply upward and trap them.
The continuation of a trend where the price successfully breaks the last structural swing level (high in an uptrend, low in a downtrend).
The break of a counter-structural swing level, indicating a potential reversal of the current trend direction.
A sideways trading range indicating that institutions are building long positions in preparation for a markup phase.
A sideways trading range indicating that institutions are offloading long positions and building short positions in preparation for a markdown phase.
A three-candle structural imbalance in price action where the first candle's wick and the third candle's wick do not overlap, leaving a gap of unfilled liquidity.
